insight: Curaçao's performance during hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup shows a mixed impact on their momentum. On average, they tend to lose a bit of momentum after a hydration break, with a mean change of -0.050 minutes per minute, indicating a slight decline in their pace or intensity. When Curaçao was pressing before a break, they were able to maintain their momentum only half of the time, suggesting that the break can sometimes disrupt their rhythm and aggression. The fact that there are no instances where Curaçao was not pressing before a break means that we cannot draw conclusions about how they perform in those situations, but overall, the breaks seem to have a slightly negative effect on their ability to sustain momentum.
